name: Comedy (Restoration of Order)
description: Social disorder, confusion, or misunderstanding is resolved through reconciliation, restoring harmony.

nodes.label: Constrained Order
nodes.definition: A social world governed by rigid rules, hierarchy, or convention. Characters are boxed in by expectations, mistaken identities, or repressive norms. The order is stable but stifling.
entry_conditions: Audience sees the social structure and its constraints on the characters
exit_conditions: A disruption introduces confusion, misunderstanding, or transgression
failure_modes: The order is too pleasant — no reason to disrupt it
Constraints are not visible, so the later liberation has no contrast
signals_in_text: Rules and expectations stated or enforced
Characters performing roles they don't fit

nodes.label: Disruption of Order
nodes.definition: An event introduces confusion into the social order — mistaken identity, a forbidden attraction, an outsider's arrival, or a scheme that goes sideways.
exit_conditions: The social order is destabilized; characters are displaced from their expected roles
typical_variants: Outsider enters and doesn't follow the rules
A plan or scheme is set in motion
failure_modes: Disruption is too mild to create real confusion
Disruption has no connection to the social constraints
signals_in_text: Characters are placed in wrong contexts

nodes.label: Escalating Chaos
nodes.definition: Misunderstandings compound, schemes collide, and characters become increasingly entangled. Each attempt to fix the confusion makes it worse.
entry_conditions: Multiple characters are now caught in the confusion
exit_conditions: The chaos reaches a peak where resolution or total disaster is imminent
typical_variants: Multiple schemes interfering with each other
Characters adopting ever more absurd disguises or lies
failure_modes: Chaos is random rather than escalating from the initial disruption
No stakes — the confusion is amusing but inconsequential
One character could easily resolve everything but doesn't for no reason

nodes.label: Dark Moment
nodes.definition: The confusion reaches a point where it threatens real harm — relationships may be permanently broken, someone may be unjustly punished, or the truth may never emerge.
entry_conditions: The confusion now threatens genuine loss
exit_conditions: A revelation, confession, or act of honesty begins to untangle the knot
typical_variants: Lovers believe they've lost each other
An innocent is about to be condemned
The scheme appears to have succeeded at someone's permanent expense
failure_modes: Dark moment is too light — no genuine threat to anything that matters
Dark moment is too dark — the tone breaks and the comedy can't recover

nodes.label: Unmasking and Recognition
nodes.definition: The truth comes out — disguises are removed, misunderstandings are explained, schemes are exposed. Characters see each other and themselves clearly.
entry_conditions: The dark moment has created enough pressure for truth to emerge
exit_conditions: All parties know the real situation
typical_variants: A third party connects the dots
failure_modes: Unmasking is arbitrary rather than triggered by the story's logic
Not all misunderstandings are addressed — loose threads remain

nodes.label: Reconciliation
nodes.definition: Characters choose to forgive, accept, and reconnect. Those who caused the confusion are pardoned (or reformed), and those who suffered it are made whole.
entry_conditions: Characters must decide how to respond
exit_conditions: Key relationships are repaired or newly formed
typical_variants: Outsider is accepted into the community
failure_modes: Forgiveness is too easy — serious harm is hand-waved
Some characters are excluded from reconciliation without reason

nodes.label: New Harmony
nodes.definition: A new social order is established — freer, more honest, or more inclusive than the original. The rigid constraints of the opening are relaxed or replaced.
entry_conditions: The original constraints have been challenged
exit_conditions: A stable, improved equilibrium is in place
typical_variants: Marriage or union celebrating the new order
New rules or attitudes replacing the old
Characters in new, more authentic roles
failure_modes: New order is identical to the old one — nothing was learned
Resolution is so thorough that the original constraints seem trivial in retrospect

edges.label: Order Broken
edges.preconditions: Social order is established; a catalyst for confusion arrives
edges.effects_on_stakes: Social stability is threatened; characters risk exposure or loss of status
edges.effects_on_character: Characters are forced out of their assigned roles
edges.common_alternatives: Disruption is accidental; disruption is deliberately engineered by a trickster
edges.anti_patterns: Disruption has nothing to do with the constraints of the opening order

edges.label: Confusion Compounds
edges.preconditions: Initial disruption is in place; characters attempt to manage it
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each fix creates new problems; the tangle deepens
edges.effects_on_character: Characters become more entrenched in false positions
edges.common_alternatives: Chaos escalates through multiple schemes colliding; chaos escalates through a single lie branching
edges.anti_patterns: Chaos is episodic rather than compounding from the original disruption

edges.label: Threatens Real Harm
edges.preconditions: Chaos has escalated enough that real relationships or well-being are at risk
edges.effects_on_stakes: Comedy momentarily becomes serious; genuine loss is possible
edges.effects_on_character: Characters realize the confusion has gone too far
edges.common_alternatives: Dark moment involves romantic loss; dark moment involves injustice to an innocent
edges.anti_patterns: Dark moment is so brief or shallow it carries no weight

edges.label: Truth Emerges
edges.preconditions: The pressure of the dark moment forces honesty or exposure
edges.effects_on_stakes: The source of confusion is eliminated; characters can see clearly
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must face each other honestly, often for the first time
edges.common_alternatives: Truth comes through confession; truth comes through accidental exposure
edges.anti_patterns: Truth emerges through a coincidence unrelated to the characters' actions

edges.label: Chooses Forgiveness
edges.preconditions: The truth is known; characters must decide how to respond
edges.effects_on_stakes: Threat of permanent damage dissolves through generosity
edges.effects_on_character: Characters grow by choosing connection over pride or punishment
edges.common_alternatives: Forgiveness is immediate; forgiveness requires a gesture of goodwill first
edges.anti_patterns: Forgiveness is automatic with no moment of decision

edges.label: Harmony Restored
edges.preconditions: Reconciliation is complete; the social order can be rebuilt
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes dissolve into celebration and stability
edges.effects_on_character: Characters occupy new, more authentic roles in the social order
edges.common_alternatives: New order is celebrated with a wedding or feast; new order is quiet but warmer
edges.anti_patterns: New harmony is identical to the old constrained order — the disruption changed nothing
